items.title: Alleged killer of 5yo girl moved to Darwin after violent clashes outside hospital

children.content: Kumanjayi Little Baby's alleged killer Jefferson Lewis, 47, has been flown to Darwin after violent clashes outside the Alice Springs hospital he had been in.

image.altText: Police steps back with gun.

articles.title: Jefferson Lewis arrested over alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Jefferson Lewis has been the subject of a police manhunt, and is accused of murdering the young girl in Alice Springs.

image.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

articles.title: Community in mourning after 5yo girl's body found

children.content: Tributes are flowing in Alice Springs for a five-year-old Aboriginal girl whose body was found earlier today, as the community mourns her death.

image.altText: Flowers on a fence

items.title: NT government accused of hiding long-term watch house detentions

children.content: Just weeks after the CLP government insisted the Northern Territory's watch house crisis was over, the NT's Aboriginal justice agency has accused it of renaming parts of a Darwin watch house to conceal that people are still being held there long term.

image.altText: Low-angle shot of Darwin city watch house building, with blue and white checkered tiles on front of building in view.

items.title: Darwin lord mayor defends proposed 5.5 per cent rates rise

children.content: In his first council budget, Darwin lord mayor Peter Styles has proposed a rates hike which he says he believes people will agree with despite no new major projects being on the cards.

image.altText: Lord Mayor Peter Styles wears a suit and looks sternly past the camera.

items.title: Cases of potentially deadly disease triple in the NT

children.content: Cases of a deadly, vaccine-preventable strain of diphtheria have now risen to 18, after the Centre for Disease Control first declared an outbreak last month.

gallery.altText: A needle injecting a vaccine into a patient's arm.

thumbnail.altText: A needle injecting a vaccine into a patient's arm.

items.title: Fossickers accuse government of breaking promise to 'restore NT lifestyle'

children.content: The Northern Territory government is proposing to introduce fees and permits for recreational fossicking, which hobbyists say breaks a key Country Liberal Party election promise.

image.altText: Two men wearing caps an sunglasses holding mattocks over shoulders

items.title: Why this First Nations film is the must-see movie of the year

children.content: Convinced to return to the story of the 2018 outback drama Sweet Country, Kaytetye filmmaker Warwick Thornton fell in love with the possibilities.

articles.title: Australia in talks over latest US proposal to open Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Australia has begun discussions with the United States about joining the latest US proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as the Trump administration tries to build an international coalition to restore freedom of navigation in the critical waterway.

image.altText: Three ships and a small dinghy on a body of water.

articles.title: 'Planning for a rainy day': How China is dealing with the global oil shock

children.content: China relies heavily on Middle Eastern oil imports, but experts say a double-insurance strategy appears to have helped the country absorb at least the initial shock after disruption to the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: An aerial view shows a crude oil tanker at an oil terminal.

children.content: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei says Tehran will eliminate "the enemies' abuses of the waterway" under new management of the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

items.title: Hard-won legacy of Australia's longest strike in jeopardy

children.content: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.
